rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component sulfonylation/arylation of styrenes with TsCl and arylboronic acids via a radical pathway
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates a sulfonyl radical (Ts•) from TsCl (or tosylate), which adds to the styrene to form a benzylic carbon radical; the aryl group from the arylboronic acid is then installed, likely via further anodic oxidation/transmetalation

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from Ts• addition to p-F-styrene
TEMPO adduct of benzylic radical (m/z 390.1539 found 390.1552)
DMPO spin adduct of benzylic carbon radical (EPR g=2.0056, AN=15.9 G, AH=21.7 G)

electron_flow: Anodic oxidation generates a sulfonyl radical from TsCl; this Ts• adds across the styrene alkene to give a benzylic C-radical, which is then coupled with the aryl group derived from the arylboronic acid to deliver the 1,2-sulfonyl-aryl product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; TEMPO-Ts adduct (calc 268.1007, found 268.1003) and TEMPO-benzylic carbon radical adduct (calc 390.1539, found 390.1552) detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: EPR spin adduct observed (g=2.0056, AN=15.9 G, AH=21.7 G), consistent with a carbon-centered radical (benzylic radical after Ts• addition)

notes: A separate Cl-radical trapping experiment using N,N-diallyl-4-methylbenzenesulfonamide with Et4NCl probes whether Cl• is generated under electrolysis (suggesting a possible parallel chlorine radical pathway from TsCl/chloride oxidation). Mechanism is consistent with anodic generation of Ts•, radical addition to styrene, then arylation via the arylboronic acid; the exact step for C–C bond formation with ArB(OH)2 (radical vs radical-polar crossover with oxidation of benzylic radical to cation) is not unambiguously assigned by the probes shown.
